name: Emotional Weight
description: Tests whether emotionally significant events automatically receive higher salience without explicit hints. A pet's cancer diagnosis should dominate over routine infrastructure work.

messages.content: Been doing a lot of infrastructure work lately. We migrated from Heroku to Kubernetes last month. It was painful but the cost savings are real — about 40% reduction in our cloud bill.
40% is substantial. The initial pain of Kubernetes is worth it once you're past the learning curve. Did you use managed K8s or set up your own cluster?
EKS on AWS. We use Terraform for all the IaC. Took about six weeks total to migrate everything, including setting up the CI/CD pipeline with ArgoCD.
EKS plus ArgoCD is a solid GitOps stack. Six weeks for a full migration is actually pretty fast.

I need to talk about something. My dog Biscuit was just diagnosed with lymphoma. The vet, Dr. Okafor at Cherry Springs Animal Hospital, says we caught it early but... it's still cancer. He's only seven.

I'm so sorry to hear about Biscuit. A cancer diagnosis for a pet is devastating, especially when they're still relatively young. It's good that Dr. Okafor caught it early — that does improve the outlook significantly for lymphoma in dogs.

Yeah, Dr. Okafor recommended a CHOP chemotherapy protocol. It's expensive — about $8,000 total — but she said dogs tolerate chemo much better than humans. Most still eat, play, wag their tails through treatment.
That's actually reassuring about how dogs handle chemo. CHOP is the gold standard for canine lymphoma. The cost is significant but having a clear treatment path helps.
We're going ahead with it. Biscuit's first treatment is next Wednesday. My wife and I are a wreck but trying to stay positive. He's been such a good boy through all the vet visits.

Biscuit sounds like a trooper. It's natural to be anxious, but the fact that you're acting quickly and following Dr. Okafor's recommendation gives him the best chance. I hope the first treatment goes smoothly.
Work update — we finished setting up monitoring with Datadog on the Kubernetes cluster. Dashboards look great. Also started using Istio for service mesh.
Datadog plus Istio gives you great observability into your K8s services. Are you using Istio's traffic management features or mainly for the telemetry?

Mainly telemetry for now, but we'll use traffic splitting for canary deployments soon. Oh — Biscuit's doing okay by the way. Two treatments in, and Dr. Okafor says the tumor is responding. He's still eating and playing fetch.

That's wonderful news about Biscuit! Tumor response after just two treatments is a really positive sign. And the fact that he's still his usual self — eating, playing — that's exactly what you hope for with the CHOP protocol.

queries.question: What's going on with their pet?
What are their biggest personal challenges right now?
What infrastructure work have they been doing?
